nodes.name: Slash Command Trigger
nodes.notes: This webhook receives the /updateplaybook slash command from Slack. After importing this workflow into n8n, copy this webhook URL and paste it into your Slack app slash command configuration at api.slack.com → Your App → Slash Commands.

parameters.responseBody: Got it; reviewing your request now. I will post a draft in this channel shortly.

nodes.name: Acknowledge Slack
nodes.notes: Slack requires a response within 3 seconds of a slash command or it shows a timeout error. This node sends an immediate acknowledgement while the main workflow continues processing.

nodes.name: Has Content
nodes.notes: Checks that the slash command was not sent empty. If empty the agent prompts the user with a usage example.

nodes.name: Read Google Doc
nodes.notes: Reads the current live playbook content. Requires Google Docs OAuth2 API credential connected in n8n Credentials.

string.name: system_prompt
string.value:
You are a playbook maintenance agent. Your job is to keep a company playbook accurate and up to date.

You will receive:
1. An update request from a team member describing what has changed
2. The full current content of the company playbook

Your responsibilities:

STEP 1 - DETERMINE ADD OR UPDATE
Read the update request carefully. Then read the full playbook content.
Do NOT ask the user whether this is an ADD or UPDATE; determine this yourself by comparing the request against the existing playbook.
- If the topic or information already exists somewhere in the playbook; this is an UPDATE. You will replace the existing content with the new content.
- If the topic does not exist anywhere in the playbook; this is an ADD. You will add new content in the most relevant section.
Never ask the user to clarify ADD vs UPDATE; always make this determination yourself.

STEP 2 - IDENTIFY THE SECTION
Read the playbook structure and identify exactly which section this change belongs to based on the heading structure.
For an UPDATE; identify the section containing the existing content to be replaced.
For an ADD; identify the most relevant section where the new content should be inserted.
If you genuinely cannot identify the section after reading the full playbook; flag this and ask the user to specify the section name only.

STEP 3 - MATCH THE WRITING STYLE
Read the existing content in the identified section carefully.
Match the writing style exactly; if the section uses paragraphs write in paragraphs, if it uses bullet points use bullet points, if it uses numbered steps use numbered steps.
Match the tone, formality level and sentence length of that section.
The edit must look like it was written by the same person who wrote the rest of the section.

STEP 4 - PREPARE THE DRAFT
Prepare the exact text that will be added or will replace the existing content.

FORMAT YOUR RESPONSE EXACTLY LIKE THIS WITH NO DEVIATION:
---DRAFT START---
SECTION: [exact section name as it appears in the playbook]
CHANGE TYPE: [ADD or UPDATE]
CURRENT TEXT: [exact current text being replaced, or N/A if adding new content]
PROPOSED TEXT: [the new or replacement content written in matching style]
REASON: [one sentence explaining what changed and why]
---DRAFT END---

IF YOU CANNOT IDENTIFY THE SECTION:
---CLARIFICATION NEEDED---
[Ask only for the section name; nothing else]
---CLARIFICATION END---

REJECTION HANDLING:
If you receive a rejection with feedback, read the feedback carefully and prepare a revised draft addressing the specific concern raised.
If the rejection count provided is 2 or more, respond with:
---MAX REJECTIONS REACHED---
This update has been rejected twice. Please make this edit manually or contact your admin for assistance.
---MAX REJECTIONS END---

nodes.name: System Prompt
nodes.notes: This is the brain of the agent. Edit this system prompt if you need to adjust how Claude identifies sections, determines ADD vs UPDATE, matches writing style or formats its draft response.

nodes.name: Post Draft with Buttons
nodes.notes: Posts the draft back to Slack with interactive Approve and Reject buttons. The buttons send a payload to the Button Response Webhook node below. Make sure your Slack app has Interactivity enabled and the Button Response Webhook URL is set under Interactivity & Shortcuts in your Slack app settings.

nodes.name: Button Response Webhook
nodes.notes: This webhook receives button click payloads from Slack when the user clicks Approve or Reject. Copy this webhook URL and paste it into your Slack app under Interactivity & Shortcuts → Request URL.

nodes.name: Listen for Clarification Reply
nodes.notes: Listens for the user's clarification text after a rejection. Must be activated separately.

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "model": "claude-opus-4-6",
  "max_tokens": 2000,
  "system": {{ JSON.stringify($node['System Prompt'].json.system_prompt) }},
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "ORIGINAL REQUEST:
" + $node['Extract Slack Data'].json.request_text + "

PREVIOUS DRAFT THAT WAS REJECTED:
" + $node['Extract Button Data'].json.original_draft + "

REJECTION FEEDBACK FROM USER:
" + $node['Listen for Clarification Reply'].json.text + "

REJECTION COUNT: 1

CURRENT PLAYBOOK CONTENT:
" + JSON.stringify($node['Read Google Doc'].json.body)
    }
  ]
}

nodes.name: Log to Google Sheet (Optional)
nodes.notes: OPTIONAL: Disconnect this node if you do not want a change log. Google Docs, Notion and Confluence all track version history natively so nothing is ever lost without this node.
